World.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
World.desc: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

World.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
World.desc: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

World.title: Trump's threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
World.desc: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

World.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
World.desc: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

World.title: Carney on verge of Liberal majority government as votes cast in three by-elections
World.desc: One more seat could solidify the Canadian PM's hold on power after several Conservatives defected.

World.title: Trump attacks Pope over criticism of Iran war
World.desc: The US president accuses Leo of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy".

Technology.title: Meta pulls Facebook ads recruiting for social media addiction lawsuits
Technology.desc: The Facebook owner recently lost a landmark social media addiction trial in California

Technology.title: OpenAI boss Sam Altman's home targeted with Molotov cocktail
Technology.desc: San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old suspect after a perimeter gate was set alight.

Technology.title: Dancer with MND performs on stage again through digital avatar
Technology.desc: Breanna Olson said brainwave tech was able to re-establish the expression and connection she felt had gone.

Technology.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
Technology.desc: The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

Technology.title: Mayor warns of London 'disinformation blizzard'
Technology.desc: Sir Sadiq Khan claims London is being targeted with disinformation portraying it as a city "in decline".

Technology.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindles, prompting user outcry
Technology.desc: The move means owners of Kindles released before 2013 will be unable to download new e-books.

Science.title: From blast off to splashdown: My days following Nasa's historic mission to the Moon
Science.desc: BBC Science Editor Rebecca Morelle reflects on how it felt to watch history being made.

Science.title: Golden eagles' return to English skies gets government backing
Science.desc: The birds could be reintroduced as early as next year following a £1m injection from the government.

Science.title: Back to Earth: What happens to the Artemis II astronauts now?
Science.desc: The astronauts will have medical checks and will be reunited with their families.

Science.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph. Now comes the hard part
Science.desc: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

Science.title: Want to help garden birds? Don't feed them in warmer months, says RSPB
Science.desc: The UK's largest bird charity has issued new guidance advising people to stop using feeders to help wildlife thrive.

Science.title: Pioneering wildlife cameraman Doug Allan dies after falling ill in Nepal
Science.desc: The cameraman and photographer won eight Emmy Awards for his work on acclaimed series like Blue Planet alongside Sir David Attenborough.
